canonicalTerms.definition: The plugin and slash command that runs multi-model deliberation loops for universal ideation. Distinct from the generic activity of brainstorming.
The structured two-round cycle (Diverge → Steer → Interrogate → Synthesize) that constitutes a brainstorm run.
Round 1 of the deliberation loop. All three model providers run in parallel, each assigned a persona, and independently produce 4-6 idea cards plus assumptions, user questions, and anti-goals.
The interactive checkpoint between Round 1 and Round 2. Claude orchestrator clusters ideas into 5-8 branches; the user selects 2-3 or applies a bias preset.
Round 2 of the deliberation loop (Socratic Interrogation). Models receive only branch summaries and output fatal risks, ranking-changing questions, and strengthening moves.
The final orchestrator phase that classifies branches into Best Bets, Wild Cards, Open Questions, and Next Experiments using deterministic support/fatal-objection counts.
The human-in-the-loop pause between Diverge and Interrogate where the user steers branch selection. Skipped in --quick mode.
A hardcoded brainstorm persona. Worldview: maximize novelty, ignore feasibility early, outsider perspective. Must produce at least one idea nobody asked for; no safe proposals.
A hardcoded brainstorm persona. Worldview: maximize execution realism, surface dependencies and adoption paths. Must name 2 concrete blockers per idea; no hand-waving.
A hardcoded brainstorm persona. Worldview: attack assumptions, propose inversions and anti-goals. Must challenge the premise itself; cannot agree without evidence.
Assignment of personas to model providers using hash(topic) % 3, preventing model-persona bias conflation.
The atomic output unit from a persona in Round 1: one proposed idea plus its assumptions, questions for the user, and anti-goals.
A cluster of related ideas produced by the orchestrator after Round 1. There are 5-8 branches per run; the user selects 2-3 for Round 2.
The number of model providers that independently proposed a similar branch in Round 1. Used as the primary convergence signal.
A blocking issue raised by a model in Round 2 that, if valid, prevents a branch from being classified as a Best Bet.
A branch that received support_count >= 2 AND fatal_objections == 0 in the synthesis step.
A branch with support_count == 1 and a high-novelty flag set. Preserved in synthesis as a low-consensus but high-originality candidate.
A branch classification for any branch with fatal_objections > 0. Listed in synthesis with the specific objections.
An explicit statement of what an idea should not achieve or optimize for, produced by a persona in Round 1 alongside the idea card.
A named automated branch-selection policy: novelty (favor novel branches), practical (favor executable branches), balanced (mixed, default for --quick).
The constraint that in Round 2 models receive only branch summaries, not full Round 1 transcripts, to preserve independence of critique.
Non-interactive run triggered by --quick flag or absence of TTY. Skips the steering checkpoint and auto-selects max-support + max-novelty + max-disagreement branches.
A diversity metric logged as telemetry only in v1. Not used for convergence detection or adaptive stopping by design.
The Claude instance that runs the Steer and Synthesize phases inline, as distinct from the subagent instances running personas in Diverge and Interrogate.
A model vendor dispatched in parallel rounds: claude (subagent), codex (codex exec --ephemeral), gemini (gemini -p). Selectable via --providers flag.
An output from Round 2 in which a model proposes how to improve or de-risk a branch rather than reject it.
